title: Unraveling the Rodeo Algorithm Through the Zeeman Model
author: FORTES, RAPHAEL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: We exploit the Pennylane and Qiskit platforms resources to unravel the Rodeo algorithm for multi-qubit Hamiltonians described by the Zeeman model in order to determine its eigenstates and eigenvalues spectrum. To this end, we improve the original method by presenting a novel methodology and techniques to define these properties for an arbitrary initial state without the need to have prior knowledge regarding the eigenvectors. First, we analyze the dynamics of a single qubit on Xanadu simulators in order to set the parameters that optimize the method performance and select the best strategies to execute the algorithm. On the sequence, we apply the methodology for a bipartite system when degeneracy and entanglement are taken into account. Finally, we compare the predictions with the results obtained on a real superconducting device provided by the IBM Q Experience program, and we establish conditions to increase the protocol efficiency for multi-qubit systems.
